message:
docs: scope SQL parity claim to SQLite-only (br-frankenpandas-m3e8)

Reality-check MEDIUM m3e8 flagged that the capability table's
"7 IO formats" row implies full pandas SQL parity — but pandas
read_sql/to_sql works with ANY SQLAlchemy engine (PostgreSQL,
MySQL, MariaDB, MS SQL, Oracle), while fp_io::read_sql / write_sql
hardcode rusqlite::Connection. There is no DBAPI2-like
SqlConnection trait, no PostgreSQL / MySQL backends, no chunksize
streaming, and coerce_float is absent. The IO detail table at
README.md:147 already had "SQLite" under the Connection column
so the existing detail was honest; the hero capability table
was the site doing the overpromising.

Two surgical README edits to make the hero match the detail:

1. Capability table (README.md:36) — split the single
   "FrankenPandas" cell for the 7 IO formats row to call out
   the SQL scope explicitly:

     Before: | Yes |
     After:  | Yes (SQL: SQLite-only via rusqlite;
              PostgreSQL/MySQL planned) |

   and likewise qualifies the pandas column to "Yes (SQL: any
   SQLAlchemy engine)" so the comparison is apples-to-apples
   rather than over-crediting pandas and under-crediting the
   Rust impl on the SQLite case.

2. Limitations table (README.md:1375) — new row spelling out
   the specific gaps (no SqlConnection trait; no PostgreSQL /
   MySQL / MS SQL / Oracle backends; no chunksize streaming;
   no coerce_float) and pointing at the follow-up epic.

Follow-up epic filed: br-frankenpandas-fd90
([EPIC] SQL backend abstraction — trait + PostgreSQL + MySQL)
— 7-slice umbrella covering the SqlConnection trait, Cargo
feature gating per backend, signature lift from
rusqlite::Connection to <C: SqlConnection>, param binding
(closes tk3k), chunksize streaming, coerce_float, and per-backend
live-oracle conformance.

No change to the IO detail table (README.md:147) or the public
API — scoping the claim is the minimum move; the actual
multi-backend work belongs on fd90.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -33,7 +33,7 @@
 | Column MultiIndex | Yes | No | Yes (foundation) |
 | Row MultiIndex | Yes | No | Scaffolded (standalone type; DataFrame row-axis integration pending — see Limitations) |
 | Categorical dtype | Yes | Yes | Yes (metadata layer) |
-| 7 IO formats (CSV/JSON/JSONL/Parquet/Excel/SQL/Feather) | Yes | Partial | Yes |
+| 7 IO formats (CSV/JSON/JSONL/Parquet/Excel/SQL/Feather) | Yes (SQL: any SQLAlchemy engine) | Partial | Yes (SQL: SQLite-only via rusqlite; PostgreSQL/MySQL planned) |
 | Conformance testing against pandas oracle | - | - | Yes (20+ packet suites) |
 
 ## Quick Example
@@ -1373,6 +1373,7 @@ Every parity report gets a **RaptorQ repair-symbol sidecar** for bit-rot detecti
 | MultiIndex not yet integrated as DataFrame row index | Foundation type exists; tracked by `frankenpandas-1zzp` | Use `set_index_multi()` for flat composite keys only |
 | Categorical metadata not propagated through arithmetic | By design (matches pandas) | Use `.cat().to_values()` to materialize |
 | No HDF5, Clipboard, or HTML IO | System-library dependencies | Use Feather (faster) or Parquet instead |
+| SQL IO is SQLite-only | `read_sql` / `write_sql` hardcode `rusqlite::Connection`; no DBAPI2 / SqlConnection trait yet; no PostgreSQL, MySQL, MS SQL, or Oracle backends; no chunksize streaming; `coerce_float` absent. Tracked by `frankenpandas-fd90` (SQL backend abstraction epic) | Use SQLite via `rusqlite::Connection::open[_in_memory]` today; export to Parquet/Feather for other DBs |
 | Single-threaded execution | No parallel execution yet | Profile-proven fast paths compensate |
 | No plotting | Requires graphics library | Export to pandas/matplotlib for visualization |
